role: Generic archetype-and-data DOM renderer that produces decision cards and composed explanatory views for the gallery, standalone pieces, and live decision surface.
capabilities: Render an archetype layout (zones and typed slots) into a DOM element from a data record
Resolve slot values via slot_map, slot.id, dotted paths, or live 'resolve:<name>' values
Render typed slots: heading, question hero, label/what-kind, lead/explanation, list, options, mark, shape, prose
Render option affordances as full-tile buttons (≤2) or ledger rows (3+) with recommended/chosen states, ordinal metal ramp, decided gold moment, write-error banner, and 'Not now' ghost
Render full-card empty/failed state moments with retry buttons
Render composed 'explained' views that nest a mockup interface beside slide content, including binary two-panel and N-option comparison layouts
Stream async resolved values into slots with a streaming indicator
Apply live DNA design tokens and spacing grammar via CSS variable injection, with cached token/grammar fetch
Wire decision takes to window.galleryBinder.decide and dispatch skip/retry CustomEvents
Provide a self-contained VI mark SVG so composed views render without unit-view.js
substance_why: It implements a complete rendering pipeline with zone/slot resolution, multiple option layouts, composed comparison views, async streaming, live DNA variable injection, and take wiring across ~600 lines of real code.
interactions_present: Option button clicks invoke window.galleryBinder.decide(address, label)
'Not now' skip button dispatches a CustomEvent 'decision:skip'
Empty/failed state retry buttons dispatch a CustomEvent 'decision:retry'
renderExplained panel CTAs invoke window.galleryBinder.decide when a decision address is present
Async 'resolve:<name>' values stream into their target slot textContent when promises resolve
el._showWriteError exposes a method to reveal the write-error banner
interactions_missing: The 'Change' button in the decided state is rendered but never wired to an action
No cancellation or cleanup for pending async resolves, risking updates to removed DOM
No keyboard/focus management or ARIA feedback beyond the error banner's aria-live
No disabled/loading state on option buttons during a take/save
No fallback UI when window.galleryBinder or org.* helper functions are absent
No retry/error UI for failed async resolves
No dynamic orientation change handling beyond the initial opts.orient class
performance_risks: First render triggers uncached fetch of /dna/tokens.json and /dna/grammar.json, blocking style resolution
Large inline SVG scene strings for binary/N-option comparisons are regenerated on every render with no caching
Async resolve promises are not cancelled, so stale resolutions may update detached DOM nodes
Whole-card innerHTML rebuilds on every data update instead of incremental updates
Repeated regex splitting and DOM queries inside render loops for every option/panel
Global _dnaResolvers promise caches fetch results indefinitely without invalidation
reuse_why: The generic slot/zone renderer and composed-view concept are worth keeping, but the file mixes too many responsibilities and relies on inline onclick wiring and global galleryBinder coupling, so it should be refactored into smaller modules.
decomposition_hints: Split the archetype card renderer (zones, slots, options, state moments, take wiring) from the composed explained-view renderer
Extract options rendering (tile, row, decided moment, ghost, error banner) into a dedicated options component
Extract SVG organism/scene helpers (planThumbnail, bars, fileStack, mesh, VI mark, binary panels) into a visual-assets module
Move async resolve/streaming logic into a cancellable resolver service
Move live DNA token/grammar injection into a theme/spacing module
Move decision-action binding (decide, skip, retry, write-error) into a separate interactions/actions module
